name: Hummus #3
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the chick peas overnight.
2. Drain and place on a cookie sheet to freeze several hours.
3. Cook the frozen beans in double the amount of water with onion, or cook in a pressure cooker instead of freezing first.
4. Blend the cooked beans (pureeing while still warm makes blending easier) and onion, using as much water as necessary to keep the blender running.
5. Add garlic cloves, soy sauce, and lemon juice to taste, along with beans.
6. If using cumin, add it now.
7. When all ingredients are blended into a thick sauce, transfer to a large bowl.
8. Add one jar of tahini and mix well, adding more tamari, garlic, or lemon juice for a stronger flavor if desired.
9. Add more water for a thinner consistency.
10. Place in a shallow dish, spread smoothly, and sprinkle with paprika and parsley.
11. Serve with pita strips, use as a sandwich spread, or stuff into celery.
Variations:
a) Avocado Hummus: Mash in 1 large ripe avocado
b) Eggplant Hummus: Mash in one medium baked eggplant (no skin) and mix with cumin added to the garbanzo tahini blend.
